topic: DeepSeek V3.2 vs Claude Sonnet 4.6 vs GPT-5 2026: Same Quality, 90% Cheaper
keyword: deepseek v3 vs claude vs gpt-5

key_points: Comprehensive pricing table: DeepSeek V3 $0.28/$0.42 vs Claude Sonnet 4.6 $3/$15 vs GPT-5 $2.50/$10 per million tokens
DeepSeek is 9x cheaper on input and 14x cheaper on output than GPT-5
Includes real cost scenarios for 10M token workloads
Side-by-side pricing for 40+ models ranked by cost
DeepSeek leads as cheapest frontier model in 2026
90% cache discounts make repeated-context workloads almost free
Hands-on benchmark comparison across MMLU, coding, math tasks
DeepSeek V3.2 achieves similar performance with GPT-5 on reasoning
Claude Sonnet 4.6 leads on SWE-bench at 79.6%
Enterprise use case cost breakdown for DeepSeek V3
Data labeling, personalization, translation workload examples
When to use each model in 2026
DeepSeek limitations: data privacy concerns due to Chinese law
Recommends multi-model strategy for most developers

stats.fact: DeepSeek V3.2 API pricing: $0.14-0.28 per million input tokens, $0.42 per million output tokens
Claude Sonnet 4.6 API pricing: $3 per million input tokens, $15 per million output tokens
GPT-5 API pricing: $2.50 per million input tokens, $10-15 per million output tokens
DeepSeek V3 is approximately 90% cheaper than GPT-5 and Claude Sonnet 4.6 on equivalent workloads
DeepSeek V3.2 SWE-bench Verified score: 72-74%, vs Claude Sonnet 4.6 at 79.6% and GPT-5 at ~80%
DeepSeek V3.2 won IMO 2025 Gold Medal (35/42), IOI 2025 Gold Medal (492/600, 10th place)
DeepSeek is banned or restricted on work devices by multiple US government agencies and European countries due to Chinese data law
At 10M personalization queries/month, Claude Opus costs $175,000 vs DeepSeek at ~$11,000 (16x cheaper)
DeepSeek V3.2 uses DSA attention mechanism reducing computational complexity from O(L²) to O(Lk), enabling 128K context at lower cost
Self-hosting DeepSeek breaks even vs API cost above 500M tokens/month; below 100M tokens, official API wins

angles: The 90% cost gap: exactly how much money you save switching to DeepSeek for common workloads
Quality parity analysis: where DeepSeek V3.2 matches GPT-5/Claude vs where it falls behind (SWE-bench, reasoning)
The data privacy trap: why enterprise teams cannot simply switch to DeepSeek despite the savings
Multi-model strategy: using DeepSeek for high-volume tasks and Claude/GPT-5 for quality-critical tasks
Self-hosting DeepSeek V3.2: when it makes financial sense vs API usage
Real cost calculator: $1M annotator budget vs $2,000 with DeepSeek API
